action.reasoning: Observation: Previous iteration with logit adjustment + focal loss collapsed to 0.1055 balanced accuracy. Validation loss was 5.3, train loss 0.007, model predicted only few-shot classes due to huge logit bias (tau*log(prior) ≈ 7-8). Analysis: The logit adjustment as implemented (subtracting tau*log(prior) from logits) was far too strong, causing the model to ignore learned features and always predict the rarest classes. The previous best (0.7137, iter 3) used simpler class-weighted CE, balanced sampler, mixup, and two-phase training without logit adjustment. Decision: Revert to that proven recipe with enhancements: use cross-entropy with effective weights and label smoothing, TrivialAugmentWide, mixup (alpha=0.5, prob=0.5), balanced sampler, cosine annealing with warmup. Remove logit adjustment completely. Run a quick 10-epoch sanity check and submit eval to confirm recovery before scaling to 80-100 epochs.
